06-REQ-005.note: Per-team heuristic default configuration covering the server-registered Drives and Preferences; per-team storage is authoritative, so a server replacement inherits it (06-REVIEW-001). The registered-by-string-id availability half is authored in bot-configuration/registry-defines-availability.

06-REQ-006.note: Per-Preference default fields: active-by-default flag and default portfolio weight.

06-REQ-007.note: Per-Drive default fields (default weight, nickname) authored in team-heuristic-defaults; the pinned-then-lexicographic-by-nickname dropdown ordering is authored in drive-management-interface; the pinnedHeuristics list's storage home is team-bot-parameters.

06-REQ-008.note: Captain-only CRUD on the heuristic default configuration (Captain-only per resolved 08-REVIEW-001).

06-REQ-009.note: Default edits never retroactively affect a game in progress; portfolios are independent of team defaults once the game has begun (launch capture).

06-REQ-010.note: Negative half of the defaults gate: no runtime other than the Captain writes heuristic defaults, enforced server-side at the function contract (data layer).

06-REQ-011.note: SPLIT: the bot parameter record is authored here — softmax temperature default, pinnedHeuristics list, and the three submission-timing fields (automatic submission time allocation, scheduled-submission interval, imminent-deadline threshold) as OPAQUE team-tunable scalars stored/edited/snapshotted by bot-configuration. The timing fields' consumption semantics (submission cadence and deadline arming) are turn-pacing's story and are deliberately NOT authored by this change; the copied-into-game-scoped-state sentence lands in bot-configuration/game-start-snapshot. Seed default values (100 ms / 50 ms) are code with design rationale in this change's design.md.

06-REQ-012.note: Negative half of the bot-parameter gate: Captain-only writes, enforced at the function contract.

06-REQ-013.note: Per-snake portfolio record contents: active Drives with concrete target/type/weight; weight overrides; Preference activation overrides; nullable temperature override.

06-REQ-014.note: Game-start initialisation from the captured defaults: active-by-default Preferences at default weight, no Drives, no overrides. dedupe:07-REQ-014.

06-REQ-015.note: The portfolio mutation set: Drive add/remove (retarget), Drive/Preference weight change, Preference activation toggle, temperature override set/clear.

06-REQ-016.note: Portfolio state persists across turns; deselection resets nothing — temperature override included, symmetric with Drives and weight overrides (06-REVIEW-005). dedupe:08-REQ-057.

06-REQ-017.note: Effective configuration = captured defaults overlaid by portfolio record, computable from persisted data without negotiation. dedupe:07-REQ-016.

06-REQ-041.note: Team-scoped bot configuration persists for the team's lifetime; archive-only team semantics (team-management/archive-not-delete) mean no cascade mechanics and no reset on archive/unarchive.

07-REQ-014.note: Framework-side statement of game-start portfolio initialisation from the captured defaults. dedupe:06-REQ-014.

07-REQ-015.note: Operator portfolio mutations take effect on the running player reactively, recomputing without restarting or clearing accumulated evaluation.

07-REQ-016.note: Framework reads the effective configuration (default overlaid by portfolio) from persisted state without further negotiation. dedupe:06-REQ-017.

07-REQ-017.note: Targets always concrete (specific snake or cell); unresolved-target Drives are never maintained in play — omitted while unresolvable, never deleted by the platform, auto re-entering when resolvable (legacy 07 Design §2.4 lead).

07-REQ-018.note: The framework never modifies team-scoped configuration on its own initiative; all team-config writes are captain/member-initiated workflows.

07-REQ-022.note: Weight changes affect scoring only — no invalidation, no new simulation; the cache-toggle machinery half stays mechanism (bot-framework's demoted internals).

07-REQ-037.note: The cheap-rescan property as the live-edit guarantee: a weight edit is pure rescoring of already-evaluated worlds. The read-cache-multiply-sum-min mechanism stays code (bot-framework design).

07-REQ-050.note: The calibration contract: lower temperature biases toward the highest-scoring direction, higher toward uniform sampling — the sign of the effect operators tune against.

07-REQ-055.note: The two temperature sources: team-wide value and per-snake override. Post-snapshot the team-side source is the game-scoped temperature value (forked from the team default at launch per game-start-snapshot).

07-REQ-056.note: Effective temperature = override else team value, derived reactively: source changes take effect at the next softmax decision with no cache invalidation and no restart.

08-REQ-008.note: UI-mirror: captain-gated affordances track current captain status reactively, effective without page reload. Authored generically (any captain-gated affordance follows a captaincy transfer), so surfaces owned by sibling capabilities inherit the pattern.

08-REQ-014.note: The team-scoped heuristic configuration page listing the team's configured heuristics; its registry-intersection classification (active/stale/registry-only) is authored in registry-defines-availability.

08-REQ-015.note: Per-Preference editing on the page: active-by-default flag and default weight.

08-REQ-016.note: Per-Drive editing on the page: default weight, nickname, pinned status and order.

08-REQ-017.note: UI-mirror of the defaults gate: mutations routed through the function contract, rejected for non-Captains, rejections surfaced; non-Captain members get the read-only view.

08-REQ-018.note: The page communicates that default edits affect future games only. Concrete affordance (banner/tooltip) stays design-level.

08-REQ-019.note: Negative: the heuristic configuration page mutates team-scoped defaults only — no per-snake or per-game state.

08-REQ-020.note: The bot parameters page: displays and edits the bot parameter record (temperature default and the submission-timing parameters).

08-REQ-021.note: UI-mirror of the bot-parameter gate: Captain-only via the function contract, read-only view for other members.

08-REQ-022.note: Parameters take effect on the next game; at game start they are copied into game-scoped values thereafter independent of the team defaults (08-REVIEW-002 launch-snapshot semantics).

08-REQ-023.note: Negative: no game-rules configuration parameter appears on the bot parameters page.

08-REQ-052.note: The Drive-type chooser with pinned-first-then-lexicographic-by-nickname ordering; its option set is the config∩registry intersection per registry-defines-availability.

08-REQ-053.note: Targeting mode per target type: eligibility-predicate-accepted targets highlighted and confirmable, the rest dimmed and inert.

08-REQ-054.note: Deterministic nearest-first keyboard cycling through eligible targets; Escape cancels without side effects. The exact three-key sort (A*-distance, clockwise angle, identity tiebreak) is demoted to code with rationale in this change's design.md — the binding residue is determinism and nearest-first.

08-REQ-055.note: Target confirmation adds the Drive at its default weight with no additional confirmation step.

08-REQ-056.note: Active Drives listed with target and weight, weight-editable and removable, edits effective immediately via the live-editing contract.

08-REQ-057.note: UI-mirror of portfolio persistence: the interface never resets Drives, overrides, or the temperature override on selection changes or turn transitions. dedupe:06-REQ-016.

08-REQ-058.note: Negative: no affordance to add a Drive outside the configured-and-registered set; registration is a code-level concern, not a runtime UI affordance.

06-REVIEW-001.note: Per-team is authoritative (Option A): heuristic defaults are per-CentaurTeam; a replacement server inherits them; the server's build-time registry determines availability only.

06-REVIEW-005.note: Temperature override persists across turns and deselection, symmetric with Drive and weight overrides (Option A).

07-REVIEW-012.note: Submission interval and imminent threshold are team-tunable bot parameters (the promotion the legacy 06-REQ-011 text adopted), not framework constants; their consumption semantics stay with the pacing story.

08-REVIEW-001.note: The load-bearing authorization split: team-scoped defaults (heuristic defaults, bot parameters) are Captain-only; game-scoped per-snake overrides (Drives, weights, activation, temperature) are editable by any current member. No timekeeper role.

08-REVIEW-002.note: Launch snapshot: all team-scoped defaults take effect only when game-scoped state is created at game start; running games are unaffected by default edits.

08-REVIEW-021.note: Registry drift handling: operable set = config ∩ build-time registry; stale config rows retained, greyed, captain-deletable, never in the in-game dropdown; new registry entries adopted via the insert-only lazy sync on a Captain visit, never overwriting Captain-edited values; the framework never writes heuristic config.
